expect: Era-level quality means (n=10 eras) form mean-reverting process, not random walk — swarm has quality attractor.
actual: CONFIRMED. Era-level Sharpe means are mean-reverting (OU process), not random walk. Full sample (n=9, 25-session eras): beta=0.215, LR_mean=8.45, VR(2)=0.599, Hurst=0.471. Mature sample (S350+, n=8): beta=0.699, LR_mean=8.78, half-life=1.9 eras (48 sessions), VR(2)=0.556. ACF(1) of first differences = -0.474 — swarm over-corrects quality changes (anti-persistent increments). 3/7 transitions are downward. Current era (S525-549) at 9.05 is above LR mean 8.78 — mean reversion predicts quality decline next era.
diff: Expected mean-reverting: CONFIRMED. Surprising: (1) anti-persistence in changes (ACF=-0.474) means swarm systematically over-corrects, (2) LR mean ~8.8 implies a quality ceiling the swarm oscillates around, (3) half-life ~48 sessions is biologically plausible (2-3x a burst window).

prediction.falsification: If next-era mean > 9.2, mean-reversion model falsified
